# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for a Node.js Express application. Use the official Node.js base image and install dependencies with npm install. The app listens on port 3000. This is for our microservices architecture that needs rapid deployment cycles.
# Category: container_security

# Using the official node image as a parent image
FROM node:current-slim

# Set the working directory in container to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Copy package.json and package-lock.json files to Docker image at /app 
COPY package*.json ./

# Install dependencies
RUN npm install

# Copy the rest of your app’s source code from your host to your image filesystem at /app
COPY . .

# Make port 3000 available outside this container
EXPOSE 3000

# Define command to run your app when the docker container starts
CMD [ "node", "server.js" ]
